title: Support SVA goto repetition [->N] in concurrent assertions
body:
## Summary

This patch adds support for the goto repetition operator `expr[->N]` in concurrent assertions, as defined in IEEE 1800-2023 Section 16.9.2. The goto repetition matches when the boolean expression occurs exactly N times, not necessarily on consecutive clock cycles. The range form `[->M:N]` remains unsupported.


## Reproducer

```systemverilog
module t;
  logic clk, req, ack;
  assert property (@(posedge clk) req[->2] |-> ack);
endmodule
```

On master:
```
%Error-UNSUPPORTED: test.sv:3:38: Unsupported: [-> boolean abbrev expression
    3 |   assert property (@(posedge clk) req[->2] |-> ack);
      |                                      ^~~
%Error-UNSUPPORTED: test.sv:3:41: Unsupported: boolean abbrev (in sequence expression)
    3 |   assert property (@(posedge clk) req[->2] |-> ack);
      |                                         ^
```

## Changes

- `verilog.y`: Add `[->N]` production in `sexpr` rule creating `AstSExprGotoRep`; remove old `[->` catch-all from `boolean_abbrev`; keep range form `[->M:N]` as UNSUPPORTED
- `V3AstNodeExpr.h`: Add `AstSExprGotoRep` node with `exprp` (boolean) and `countp` (repetition count)
- `V3Width.cpp`: Type-check expr as boolean, propagate count width, set result to bit
- `V3AssertPre.cpp`: Lower `[->N]` to counter-based loop via `createGotoRepPExpr()` -- counts N occurrences of `$sampled(expr)` across clock cycles; handle both standalone and implication-antecedent contexts; add `validateGotoRepCount()` helper for error checking
- `V3EmitV.cpp`: Add `visit(AstSExprGotoRep*)` for `--lint-only` / `--xml-only` re-emission

## Test

- Added `test_regress/t/t_assert_goto_rep.{v,py}` -- functional test with N=1/2/3, overlapping (`|->`), and non-overlapping (`|=>`) implications
- Added `test_regress/t/t_assert_goto_rep_bad_count.{v,py,out}` -- error test for non-constant and zero count
- Updated `test_regress/t/t_sequence_sexpr_unsup.out` -- removed `[->` unsupported messages
- Updated `test_regress/t/t_property_sexpr_parse_unsup.out` -- removed `[->` unsupported messages

---
Developed by PlanV GmbH, assisted with Claude Code.

Reviewed by YilouWang.
